all.en: Welcome to the company, and congratulations on joining our growing global team.
all.zh: 歡迎加入公司，恭喜您成為我們不斷擴展的全球團隊的一員。

all.en: This orientation guide will help you settle into your role during the first two weeks.
all.zh: 本份新人手冊將協助您在最初兩週順利適應您的職位。

all.en: Your onboarding journey begins with a formal welcome session on your first Monday morning.
all.zh: 您的新人入職旅程將從到職第一個星期一上午的正式歡迎會開始。

all.en: The welcome session takes place in the main auditorium and starts promptly at nine o'clock.
all.zh: 歡迎會於主會議廳舉行，將於上午九點準時開始。

all.en: Please bring a valid government-issued identification document for the badge issuance process.
all.zh: 請攜帶有效的政府核發身分證件，以便辦理識別證手續。

all.en: Your manager will meet you after the session to introduce you to your immediate team.
all.zh: 會議結束後，您的主管將與您會面，並向您介紹直屬團隊成員。

all.en: A dedicated buddy will be assigned to support you throughout your initial onboarding period.
all.zh: 我們會指派一位專屬夥伴在您整個新人階段提供協助。

all.en: Your buddy can answer general questions and help you navigate internal tools and processes.
all.zh: 您的夥伴可以回答一般性問題，並協助您熟悉內部工具與流程。

all.en: On Tuesday, you will attend training on company values, ethics, and the code of conduct.
all.zh: 星期二您將參加公司價值觀、倫理與行為準則的訓練課程。

all.en: Wednesday focuses on benefits enrollment, payroll setup, and tax documentation.
all.zh: 星期三的重點為福利申請、薪資設定與稅務文件辦理。

all.en: Please review the benefits handbook in advance so you can make informed selections.
all.zh: 請事先閱讀福利手冊，以便您能做出明智的選擇。

all.en: Thursday is reserved for IT setup, including laptop configuration and account provisioning.
all.zh: 星期四將安排 IT 設定，包括筆電配置與帳號開通。

all.en: You will receive an email with login credentials and a temporary password before noon.
all.zh: 您將於中午前收到一封載有登入帳號與臨時密碼的電子郵件。

all.en: Please change your password immediately and enable multi-factor authentication right away.
all.zh: 請立即更改密碼，並馬上啟用多因素驗證機制。

all.en: Friday concludes the first week with an interactive office tour and a team lunch.
all.zh: 星期五以互動式辦公室導覽與團隊午餐結束第一週。

all.en: During the second week, you will begin shadowing experienced colleagues in your department.
all.zh: 第二週起，您將開始觀摩部門內資深同事的工作。

all.en: Shadowing is a great way to learn workflows, tools, and team communication styles.
all.zh: 觀摩學習是了解工作流程、工具與團隊溝通方式的絕佳方法。

all.en: Take notes during your sessions and prepare any questions for your weekly check-ins.
all.zh: 請於觀摩期間做筆記，並準備好您每週會議要提出的問題。

all.en: Weekly one-on-one meetings with your manager will be scheduled every Friday afternoon.
all.zh: 每週與主管的一對一會議將安排於每週五下午。

all.en: These meetings are an opportunity to share progress, raise concerns, and set short-term goals.
all.zh: 這些會議是您分享進度、提出疑慮並設定短期目標的好機會。

all.en: By the end of week two, you should feel comfortable handling everyday responsibilities.
all.zh: 在第二週結束前，您應能勝任日常工作職責。

all.en: Remember that learning continues well beyond the initial orientation period.
all.zh: 請記得學習是長期的，並不會在新人階段結束後就停止。

all.en: Our internal learning platform offers hundreds of courses, certifications, and live workshops.
all.zh: 公司內部學習平台提供數百項課程、認證與線上工作坊。

all.en: Once again, welcome aboard, and we look forward to your contributions in the months ahead.
all.zh: 再次歡迎您的加入，期待您在未來數月內貢獻所長。

all.en: A thirty-day performance check-in will be conducted by your manager to discuss your initial accomplishments and learning needs.
all.zh: 您的主管將於到職滿三十天時進行績效檢核，討論初期成就與學習需求。

all.en: Following that, ninety-day and six-month reviews will help align your career goals with departmental priorities.
all.zh: 後續還會安排九十天與六個月的評估，協助將您的職涯目標與部門重點對齊。

all.en: Employee resource groups offer networking opportunities and a welcoming community for new colleagues across all locations.
all.zh: 員工資源社群為各地的新進同仁提供建立人脈的機會與溫暖的社群氛圍。

all.en: Membership in these groups is entirely voluntary, and meetings are often held during lunch hours or virtually.
all.zh: 加入這些社群完全自願，會議多於午餐時間或線上舉行。

all.en: The company also sponsors mentorship programs that pair early-career employees with experienced senior leaders.
all.zh: 公司亦贊助導師計畫，由經驗豐富的資深主管與初階員工進行配對。

all.en: Applications for the mentorship program open twice a year and are reviewed by a dedicated selection committee.
all.zh: 導師計畫每年開放申請兩次，由專責遴選委員會進行審查。

all.en: Please take some time to explore the wellness benefits, which include fitness reimbursements and counseling services.
all.zh: 敬請花些時間了解員工健康福利，包括健身費用補助與諮商服務。

all.en: Mental health support is confidential and available to all employees and their immediate family members at no cost.
all.zh: 心理健康支援採保密形式，全體員工及其直系家屬皆可免費使用。

all.en: We encourage you to embrace every learning opportunity and to ask questions whenever something feels unclear.
all.zh: 我們鼓勵您把握每一個學習機會，遇到任何不清楚之處皆可隨時提問。

all.en: Your fresh perspective is highly valued, and we are excited to see the impact you will bring to our organization.
all.zh: 我們高度重視您嶄新的視角，期待見證您為組織帶來的影響力。

all.en: An onboarding checklist will be shared with you on day one to help track your progress through key milestones.
all.zh: 報到第一天將提供新人入職檢核表，協助您追蹤完成各項關鍵里程碑的進度。

all.en: Building access cards must be worn visibly at all times while inside any company facility for safety and identification purposes.
all.zh: 為確保安全與身分辨識，於任何公司場所內皆須將門禁卡明顯配戴於身上。

all.en: Reimbursement for commuting expenses, where applicable, is administered through the same expense system used for business travel.
all.zh: 如有適用，通勤費用補助將透過與商務差旅相同的費用報銷系統辦理。

all.en: The company sponsors quarterly volunteer days, and employees may use up to four paid hours per quarter for community service.
all.zh: 公司每季舉辦志工日，員工每季可運用最多四小時帶薪時間參與社區服務。

all.en: Tuition assistance is available for approved professional certifications and degree programs related to your current or future role.
all.zh: 員工進修補助適用於與目前或未來職務相關且經核准的專業認證與學位課程。

all.en: Office attire is business casual unless your role requires specialized clothing or you are meeting with external clients.
all.zh: 辦公室服儀以商務休閒為原則，除非您的職務需特殊服裝或將與外部客戶會面。

all.en: Cafeteria meal cards are issued upon completion of payroll setup and may be reloaded conveniently through the employee portal.
all.zh: 員工餐廳餐卡將於薪資設定完成後核發，並可透過員工入口網站便利地儲值。

all.en: Sustainability is a core company value, and we encourage all new employees to participate in our paperless workflow initiatives.
all.zh: 永續是公司的核心價值，我們鼓勵全體新進員工共同參與無紙化工作流程計畫。

all.en: Open-door office hours are scheduled with senior leadership every other Wednesday, providing opportunities for direct dialogue and feedback.
all.zh: 資深主管隔週三設有開放時段，提供同仁直接對話與意見回饋的機會。

all.en: Once again, welcome to the team, and please never hesitate to ask for support as you settle into your new role.
all.zh: 再次歡迎您加入團隊，於您熟悉新職務的過程中，請隨時尋求協助，切勿猶豫。

all.en: Honestly, my biggest tip for your first week is to write down every name you hear, because trying to remember twelve new faces from memory alone is basically impossible.
all.zh: 老實說，我給你第一週最大的建議就是把每個聽到的名字都寫下來，因為光憑記憶要記住十二張新面孔幾乎是不可能的。

all.en: When my buddy took me to lunch on day three, she asked what hobbies I had outside of work, and that one casual question genuinely helped me feel less anonymous.
all.zh: 當我的 buddy 在第三天帶我去吃午餐時，她問了我工作之外有什麼興趣，這個隨口一問的問題真的讓我覺得自己不再是個隱形人。

all.en: If you get lost looking for a meeting room, just ask anyone in the hallway because honestly the building layout confuses people who have worked here for years.
all.zh: 如果你找會議室迷路了，隨便找走廊上的任何人問就好，因為老實說這個建築物的格局連在這工作多年的人都會搞混。

all.en: Don't worry if you forget how to log into a tool on day five and need to ask again, since most of us did the exact same thing during our first month.
all.zh: 如果你到第五天還忘了怎麼登入某個工具而需要再問一次，別擔心，我們大部分人在剛來的第一個月都做過同樣的事。

all.en: The vending machine on the third floor has the best snacks, but please bring small coins because the card reader has been broken since last summer.
all.zh: 三樓的自動販賣機零食最好吃，但請帶些零錢，因為刷卡機從去年夏天就壞了一直沒修。

all.en: I made the mistake of skipping the benefits handbook in my first week, and then spent three months wondering why my health insurance card never arrived.
all.zh: 我第一週懶得看福利手冊，結果接下來三個月都在納悶為什麼我的健保卡一直沒送到。

all.en: If you ever feel overwhelmed during your first month, please tell your manager honestly, because most of us prefer to know early rather than hear about it after a quiet meltdown.
all.zh: 如果你在第一個月任何時候覺得壓力大，請誠實告訴你的主管，因為大部分主管寧可早知道，也不想等到你默默崩潰之後才聽說。

all.en: By the way, the coffee in the kitchenette is fine, but the cafe across the street really is worth the five-minute walk if you have the time.
all.zh: 順帶一提，茶水間的咖啡還行，但對街那間咖啡廳真的值得你花五分鐘走過去，如果你有時間的話。

all.en: Last year a new hire started a weekly walking group during lunch, and now twelve of us join him every Wednesday, regardless of department or seniority.
all.zh: 去年一位新進同仁發起了午休散步團，現在我們有十二個人每週三都會跟著他走，不分部門也不分資歷。

all.en: Honestly, the best advice I ever received was to say yes to every coffee chat in your first month, because those casual conversations become the friendships that carry you through tough quarters.
all.zh: 老實說，我聽過最棒的建議就是第一個月所有的咖啡邀約都答應，因為那些隨意的閒聊會變成在艱難的季度裡陪你撐下去的友誼。
